12.reason: [Array index out of bounds: when x = len-1, A[x+1] accesses A[len], which is beyond the valid range if A has size len+1 and indices 1..len are used. Also, loop may go below index 1 without bound check.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [When x becomes -1, A[x] accesses invalid memory (out-of-bounds). The loop condition should also check x >= 0 to prevent underflow.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Array index out of bounds: when x = len-1, A[x+1] accesses A[len], which is beyond the valid range if A has size len+1 and indices 1..len are used. Also, loop may go below index 1 without bound check.]

16.code: if(x>=1) //若x存在则说明该排列目前不是最后一个：n到1
16.reason: [This condition should be x >= 0, not x >= 1. When n=1, the initial permutation is [1], and x starts at 0; but since there's no next permutation, it should return 0. However, for n>=2, the first valid x can be 0 (e.g., [1,2] -> x=0). So x>=0 is correct condition to proceed.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition should be x >= 0, not x >= 1. When n=1, the initial permutation is [1], and x starts at 0; but since there's no next permutation, it should return 0. However, for n>=2, the first valid x can be 0 (e.g., [1,2] -> x=0). So x>=0 is correct condition to proceed.]

19.reason: [Array index out of bounds: A[y] with y = len is valid only if A has at least len+1 elements and uses 1-based indexing, but loop may decrement y below x without sufficient bound check.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [A is indexed from 1 to n in main(), but here y starts at len (which is n), so A[y] is A[n], which is valid only if array A has size at least n+1. However, more critically, when x is 0 and n=1, this part shouldn't execute—but due to wrong condition above, it might. Also, if x is -1 (from previous loop), this causes undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Array index out of bounds: A[y] with y = len is valid only if A has at least len+1 elements and uses 1-based indexing, but loop may decrement y below x without sufficient bound check.]
